run_summary: Portfolio up +2.4% on mark-to-market; all four open positions remain intact with original catalysts unresolved. NTLA and PTCT show strong unrealized gains, while ALK and ALGN are slightly underwater. New flag batch features four semi/semi-cap names (ONTO, AEIS, VECO) in a volatile sector tape, one defense fade (AVAV—re-entry warning applies), and one benign post-earnings pop (FIZZ). AVAV re-entry is rejected despite fresh flag due to insufficient confidence gain and no new information vs. exit rationale. ONTO and VECO pass the exploratory bar; others SKIP or WATCH.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semi sector strength (AVGO +4.8%, NVDA +3.6%, SMH +2%) pulling laggards
entries.thesis: ONTO is down heavily over five days but only bouncing modestly today while SMH is up ~2% and AVGO up ~5%. If the semi rally has legs and the prior drawdown was ONTO-specific (guide cut / earnings) that's already known, the sympathy catch-up is under-reflected. No new 8-K, so today's bounce is likely early beta re-engagement — usually there's follow-through when a beaten-down semicap starts to move with the group.
entries.what_kills: A confirmed company-specific overhang (customer concentration issue at a memory/logic customer, guide cut still in effect) would justify persistent underperformance; SMH rally reversing
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (4 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: UNDERDONE conf 3 with a coherent semi-cyclical catalyst: beaten-down semicap (-22.99% over 5 days) bouncing modestly (+4.12% today) while the broader SMH rallies +2% and AVGO up ~5%. Thesis is that ONTO-specific overhang (likely prior guidance cut) is known, and sympathy participation is under-reflected on first-day beta re-engagement. Setup has asymmetry: if SMH rally continues and ONTO gap-fills toward peers, the bounce extends; if SMH reverses, the fade is confirmed. Low volume multiple (0.84) on prior drawdown suggests passive/forced selling, not fresh institutional distribution. This fits the exploratory tier: real setup with a catalyst thread (semi strength), coherent thesis, but not overwhelming conviction—want to test whether beaten-down semi-cap sympathy plays actually work as described.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes ONTO's prior overhang is 'known' and already priced in, but provides no evidence (no 8-K cited, no specific guidance cut quantified) that the market has actually digested this negative catalyst.
red_team.critique: The bull case rests on the premise that ONTO's 22.99% five-day decline was ONTO-specific (guidance cut) and is now 'known,' leaving only sympathy participation to be filled. However, the flag itself states 'No new 8-K, so today's bounce is likely early beta re-engagement'—which means no public confirmation of what caused the drawdown. Without naming the catalyst that caused the 23% drop, the thesis conflates sector momentum (SMH +2%, AVGO +5%) with a belief that a company-specific problem has been resolved or priced. If the drawdown was driven by forward guidance, unresolved customer concentration, or margin concerns that the market hasn't yet front-run, today's 4.12% bounce could be a false start before re-acceleration of the decline. The low volume multiple (0.84) is cited as evidence of passive selling, but it equally supports the read that institutional buyers have not yet engaged—raising the question of whether they will.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No identified catalyst; possible customer concentration or memory capex fear
entries.thesis: VECO down while SMH +2%, TSM +4%, AVGO +3.7% is a striking divergence. Stock is down ~23% over five sessions with no 8-K and no earnings imminent — this is either late-cycle capitulation on an already-broken chart or specific customer/OEM concern (Veeco sells to memory/advanced packaging). Against this semi tape, if there's no company-specific bad news, the disconnect creates asymmetric bounce potential. Low volume (0.62x) on today's drop suggests forced/passive selling rather than fresh negative info.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K, preannouncement, or news of a major customer (Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC) cutting orders would validate the underperformance as fundamental. Also: if ASML/AMAT commentary this week flagged advanced packaging or MOCVD weakness, VECO drop is justified.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (4 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 with a clear technical divergence: VECO down -23.3% over 5 days while SMH +2%, TSM +4%, AVGO +3.7%; no 8-K, no earnings imminent, low volume (0.62x) on the drop. Thesis is that this is either forced/passive selling or specific customer concern (memory capex), but the scale of divergence creates asymmetric bounce if no 8-K surfaces. What_kills is well-articulated: if an 8-K/preannouncement emerges or a major customer (Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC) is disclosed cutting orders, the drop is fundamental and justified. This is a classic exploratory setup: real technical divergence, coherent thesis with a named kill switch, but doesn't meet conviction bar due to lack of positive catalyst (no 8-K, no news—just divergence). Exploratory 6% position allows testing whether small-cap semi-cap divergence plays actually compress as described.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis relies on the absence of an 8-K to infer the 23.3% drop is forced/passive selling, but absence of disclosure is not evidence of absence of fundamental damage.
red_team.critique: The bull case hinges on a technical argument: VECO down 23.3% while SMH +2% and peers hold is 'asymmetrically' overdone, and low volume (0.62x) proves it's not informed selling. However, the 'what_kills' section explicitly flags that an 8-K disclosing customer concentration or capex cuts would validate the underperformance, and that ASML/AMAT commentary on memory or packaging weakness could justify VECO's drop. The problem is that the absence of an 8-K as of the date of this analysis does not mean no company-specific damage exists—it may mean the damage (customer order visibility, architectural shifts in advanced packaging) simply hasn't triggered a formal disclosure threshold yet. Memory and packaging end-markets are notoriously binary; a large customer's capex pause can take weeks to surface in filings. The low volume on the drop argues for illiquidity, not innocence of the underlying thesis. Without positive catalyst (earnings, customer reaffirmation, or peer commentary supporting demand) the trade is betting against a divergence that may be rational.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No specific catalyst found; group-wide defense weakness with no news thread
entries.thesis: AVAV down hard despite still being up sharply over 5 days and against a geopolitically supportive backdrop (Iran oil sanctions restored, Greenland/NATO headlines). No 8-K, no earnings, no company news — this reads as profit-taking on an extended run rather than fundamental deterioration. Fade candidate if defense tape stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a specific negative catalyst (contract loss, DoD budget headline hitting drone programs); if the sell-off continues on rising volume tomorrow, it's likely institutional distribution not just noise
entries.reasoning: Re-entry flag carries a re-entry warning: you closed AVAV 12 days ago for a -4.75% realized loss on UNDERDONE conf 4. Your exit explicitly noted the entry conviction was flawed due to earnings whipsaw risk and inability to verify 8-K scope at the time. Today's flag is OVERDONE conf 3 (down -8.09% today, up +16.93% over 5 days). This flag proposes a profit-taking fade on extended rally with no new catalyst. To re-enter, two conditions must hold: (1) confidence >= 4 (this flag is conf 3, so fails the bar), and (2) genuinely new information (the thesis is 'profit-taking on an extended run'—this is a continuation of the same sector/momentum analysis, not new information). Even if I wanted to re-enter, conf 3 on a recently-closed loss prevents it. WATCH status allows for re-evaluation if fresh evidence (company 8-K, analyst downgrade, short interest spike) emerges.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Samsung earnings miss triggering semi-cap sector derating
entries.thesis: AEIS derives significant revenue from semiconductor capital equipment (power delivery for etch/deposition tools). A ~17% five-day drawdown alongside broader semi-cap weakness is consistent with sector derating. Magnitude looks appropriate given the exposure and prior valuation.
entries.what_kills: Reaffirmation of 2026 semi WFE from major foundry customers; AEIS data-center/industrial segments offsetting semi weakness
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 on a -17.35% 5-day drawdown tied to Samsung earnings and broader semi-cap sector derating. Thesis is that AEIS, being semi-equipment power delivery exposed, is fairly valued on the derating—the move looks 'appropriate given the exposure and prior valuation.' This is NOT a mispricing call; it's a sanity check that the move is rational. If the move is already rational, there is no alpha to extract. No catalyst URL provided. The flag itself notes this is 'appropriate' derating, not an overdone or underdone call—it's just a description of why the stock fell. Without a contrarian view (either that the semi derating is overdone or that AEIS has hidden upside from data-center/industrial segments), this is not a trade.

entries.name: National Beverage Corp.
entries.thesis: Earnings 8-K filed 7/2 (items 2.02 + 8.01) two days after the print, stock +7.5% on 2.6x volume — normal post-earnings response for a low-float, low-coverage name. Move looks proportional; no obvious mispricing.
entries.what_kills: Reversal on discovery that the 8.01 item contained a negative disclosure (guidance withdrawal, litigation).
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 on post-earnings pop: +7.5% on 2.6x volume, 8-K filed 7/2 with items 2.02 and 8.01. Thesis explicitly states the move 'looks proportional; no obvious mispricing.' This is a flag that correctly identifies the move as explained and fair. No contrarian setup, no catalyst to drive further move, no basis for entry. The flag's own thesis argues against a trade.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector-wide housing/rate weakness; Fed minutes showing hawkish split
entries.thesis: BLDR is down sharply over five days with no 8-K and average volume today — the move looks like continuation of a rate-driven housing derating (Fed minutes showed split on cuts, XLRE -1.65%, XLB -2.6%). The magnitude over 5 days is large but proportional to a housing demand scare; today's leg is just tape-following. Nothing here suggests mispricing either direction.
entries.what_kills: A specific negative company catalyst (guide cut, 8-K) emerging would recharacterize this as UNDERDONE bearish; conversely a rate-cut surprise would make it OVERDONE

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly sector rotation or analyst note not in feed
entries.thesis: ALGN is down sharply on a day when XLV is -1.3% and XLY is -1.78% — some sector explanation but the magnitude exceeds sector move. Volume is BELOW average, however, which argues against a discovered catalyst; more like thin-tape reaction. No 8-K. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+ — bear case needs a whisper/note we don't have; bull case (fade the move) needs a reason it's overdone that isn't just 'no news.'
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a specific negative catalyst (guide-down, competitive threat news) would break the UNCLEAR framing toward RATIONAL bearish

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
entries.catalyst: No visible catalyst; outperforming XBI meaningfully
entries.thesis: BEAM up 7%+ on above-average volume with no 8-K and no earnings scheduled. XBI is up 1.9% so some sector tailwind exists, but BEAM is outperforming the sector by 5%+ which suggests something more specific — either a data leak, an unnoticed press release, or partnership speculation. Without concrete evidence I can't articulate either bull or bear at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Simple sector rotation with no company-specific news over the next few sessions

entries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown; possible read-through from Trump/Walmart beef price commentary or an unreported analyst action
entries.thesis: TXRH is down while XLY is up nearly 1%, so this is name-specific not sector. No 8-K, no earnings imminent per catalyst_signals. Trump's post about Walmart cutting ground beef prices ~15% is a potential thread — beef is TXRH's core input cost, so lower beef could actually be BULLISH for margins, not bearish. Cannot construct a confident directional read from available evidence.

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: New material agreement + financial obligation 8-K on 6/30, plus sector selloff
entries.thesis: LSCC filed an 8-K on 6/30 with items 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (new financial obligation) — likely a debt raise or credit facility. Combined with the SMH -4.5% tape, the stock is down mid-single digits. A refinancing/new debt facility is usually not a material valuation event for a company of LSCC's size, so a portion of today's drop is likely sector-driven overshoot rather than filing-driven. Low confidence because the 1.01 could also be an acquisition financing (bearish if pricey) — the exhibit needs reading.
entries.what_kills: The 1.01 turns out to be an expensive acquisition, dilutive convert, or covenant-loosening amendment that signals stress; semi weakness broadens.

entries.name: Kratos Defense
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with AVAV earnings + broad defense strength
entries.thesis: KTOS is riding the same drone/defense narrative as AVAV, with no own 8-K. Five-day return ~20% suggests a lot has already been priced in, but with ITA +1.8% and defense as a Trump-friendly sector, momentum can persist. No clear mispricing to trade against.
entries.what_kills: Geopolitical thaw; a KTOS-specific negative headline; profit-taking after such a strong run.

entries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Unknown — sector strength plus unidentified company-specific driver
entries.thesis: CRSP +8% with XBI +2.5% is roughly 3x sector beta, suggesting some company-specific catalyst exists but is not in the SEC feed (could be conference presentation, analyst note, or Casgevy commercial data leak). Without identifying the driver, I cannot commit to overdone or underdone at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify catalyst first
